ddt:Target.Name: C1A-HS_CGW_SW3_DoIP_v1.0
ddt:Target.Description:
2019/01/14: J. Duretz Creation of the DataBase, based on SW2
2019/09/10: J. Duretz Update of the DataBase, for SW3.x Full Database available on demand with additional Security Data / Screens.

Item.Text: Transfer disabled from DLC and EXT
ID monitor of CAN monitor START
BUS monitor of CAN monitor START
No bus is requested to be monitored
Token allocated to DLC Plug DoIP
Token allocated to DLC Plug DoCAN
Token allocated to Remote Diag DoIP
Token allocated to Remote Diag DoCAN

Data.Name: SessionName
Data.Comment: Diagnostic session values. See documentation for more information.
Data.Description:
[0x00] : ISO / SAE Reserved
[0x01] : Default session
[0x02] : Programming session
[0x03] : Extended diagnostic session
[0x04] : Safety system diagnostic session
[0x05 - 0x3F] : ISO / SAE Reserved
[0x40 - 0x5F] : Vehicle manufacturer specific
[0x60 - 0x7E] : System supplier specific
[0x7F] : ISO / SAE Reserved

Data.Name: DTCFormatIdentifier
Data.Comment: DTC format - Renault/Nissan use value 0 (3 bytes DTC)

Item.Text: Device and failure type ODB codding
circuit short to ground or open
circuit short to battery or open
signal rate of change below threshold
signal rate of change above threshold
signal bias level out of range / zero adj. failure
signal has too few transitions / events
signal has too many transitions / events
alive / sequence counter incorrect / not updated
value of signal protection calculation incorrect
Component or sys. operation obstructed or blocked
Fuel Volume Regulator Control Circuit Range/Performance
Fuel Volume Regulator Control Circuit Low
Fuel Volume Regulator Control Circuit High
Fuel Shutoff Valve "A" Control Circuit Low
Fuel Shutoff Valve "A" Control Circuit High

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerSparePartNumber
Data.Comment: Ordered Part Number - This value shall contain the number of the part ordered by OEM
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the vehicle manufacturer spare part number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierIdentifier
Data.Comment: Name and address information of the supplier.
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier name and address information.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: ECUSerialNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the ECU (server) serial number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific.

Data.Name: VIN
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the VIN number.

Record data content and format shall be specified by the vehicle manufacturer.

It shall not be erased when the ECU is reprogrammed.

Data.Name: vehicleManufacturerECUHardwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used by reading services to reference the vehicle-manufacturer-specific ECU (server) hardware number.

Record data content and format shall be serverspecific and defined by vehicle manufacturer.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Data.Name: systemSupplierECUSoftwareVersionNumber
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the system supplier specific ECU (server) software version number.

Record data content and format shall be server specific and defined by the system supplier.

Item.Text: Menu package integrity check in progress
Gateway package integrity check in progress
Refuse to sleep due to FOTA
Sleep or Crank fault, Disable DiagMuxDTC
Menu package Integrity check in progress
Gateway package Integrity check in progress

Data.Name: diagnosticVersion
Data.Description:
This value shall be used to reference the diagnostic version of the ECU.

It is defined by the vehicle manufacturer.

Refer to manufacture ECU designer.

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Default
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Programming
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Request.Name: ClearDiagnosticInformation.All
Request.Comment: The ClearDiagnosticInformation service is used by the tool to clear diagnostic information in an ECUs memory

Request.Name: ReadDTCInformation.ReportDTC
Request.Comment: DDT2000 Failures page requests / DDT : Unusable for Clip import

Request.Name: TesterPresent.WithResponse
Request.Comment: This request enable P2P tester present for tool (Screen, IOctl, ...)

Request.Name: StartDiagnosticSession.Extended
Request.Comment: Standard definition for DDT2000 automatic screen

Test.Description: CAN interface (CAN controller hardware) is failed
The CGW Monitoring mode is active

CAN communication failure with BCM sending following signals: <V_WakeUpSleepCommand>, <GADE_v2>, <AbsoluteTimeSince1rstIgnition>, <VehicleStates>, <DeliveryModeInformation_v2> and <PWTstartForbiddenFeedback_FOTA>
CAN communication failure with VDC sending following signals: <DistanceTotalizer>and <VehicleSpeed>
CAN communication failure with USM sending following signals: <BatteryVoltage> and <IgnitionSupplyConfirmation>
CAN communication failure with HEVC sending following signals: <HVFOTAConditionCheckStatus> and <UserSOC>
CAN communication failure with ECM sending the following signal: <RawSensor>

Label.Text: Transfert Disable Status by Bus Load Filter
Vehicle Manufacturer Kit Assembly Part Number (F18E)
System Supplier ECU SW Version Number (F195)
Mileage Last Mode Change to DiagCom
Mileage Last Mode Change to Intermediate
Mileage Last Repro Execution Condition Violation DLC
Mileage Last Repro Execution Condition Violation DoIP
Mileage Last IP Packet Filter DoIP
Mileage Last IP Packet Filter Ext
Mileage Last DoIP Payload Filter DoIP
Mileage Last DoIP Payload Filter Ext
Step 1: Start Diag Session by clicking on START button
Step 2: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 3: Select "DiagCom Mode STOP" and click on WRITE button
Step 1: Start Diag Session by clicking on START button
Step 2: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 3: Select "DiagCom Mode START" and click on WRITE button
Step 1: Start Diag Session by clicking on START button
Step 2: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 3: If CGW status is "Diag Com Mode", then skip this step
Else, Choose "Diag Com Mode START" and ckick on WRITE button
Step 4: Select CAN network to monitor and click on WRITE button
Step 1: Start Diag Session by clicking on START button
Step 2: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 1: Start Diag Session by clicking on START button
Step 2: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 3: If CGW status is "Diag Com Mode", then skip this step
Else, Choose "Diag Com Mode START" and ckick on WRITE button
Step 4: Select Ethernet sniffer port (Port from which you want to receive traffic)
Step 1: Start Diag Session by clicking on START button
Step 2: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 5: Select Ethernet capture port (Port you want to monitor traffic)
Step 6: Select the mirroring mode (Ingress / Egress traffic only or both)
CAN ID Monitoring Mode - Table 1 Configuration
RX Network: Select the source bus from which the frame is coming
Step 3: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 4: If CGW status is "Diag Com Mode", then skip this step
Else, Choose "Diag Com Mode START" and ckick on WRITE button
Step 7: Go to Activation Tab to start ID Monitoring mode
CAN ID: To enter a CAN ID with 11 bits, use following format: 000004D8
CAN ID: To enter a CAN ID with 29 bits, use following format 18DAF1D4
Step 1: Fill-up the Table 1 with all CAN IDs you want to monitor
Step 6: Go to "Table 2 Configuration" if you need to configure more CAN IDs
CAN ID Monitoring Mode - Table 2 Configuration
RX Network: Select the source bus from which the frame is coming
Step 3: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 4: If CGW status is "Diag Com Mode", then skip this step
Else, Choose "Diag Com Mode START" and ckick on WRITE button
Step 7: Go to Activation Tab to start ID Monitoring mode
CAN ID: To enter a CAN ID with 11 bits, use following format: 000004D8
CAN ID: To enter a CAN ID with 29 bits, use following format 18DAF1D4
Step 1: Fill-up the Table 2 with all CAN IDs you want to monitor
Step 6: Go to "Table 3 Configuration" if you need to configure more CAN IDs
CAN ID Monitoring Mode - Table 3 Configuration
RX Network: Select the source bus from which the frame is coming
Step 3: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 4: If CGW status is "Diag Com Mode", then skip this step
Else, Choose "Diag Com Mode START" and ckick on WRITE button
Step 7: Go to Activation Tab to start ID Monitoring mode
CAN ID: To enter a CAN ID with 11 bits, use following format: 000004D8
CAN ID: To enter a CAN ID with 29 bits, use following format 18DAF1D4
Step 1: Fill-up the Table 3 with all CAN IDs you want to monitor
Step 6: Go to "Table 4 Configuration" if you need to configure more CAN IDs
CAN ID Monitoring Mode - Table 4 Configuration
RX Network: Select the source bus from which the frame is coming
Step 3: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 4: If CGW status is "Diag Com Mode", then skip this step
Else, Choose "Diag Com Mode START" and ckick on WRITE button
Step 7: Go to Activation Tab to start ID Monitoring mode
CAN ID: To enter a CAN ID with 11 bits, use following format: 000004D8
CAN ID: To enter a CAN ID with 29 bits, use following format 18DAF1D4
Step 1: Fill-up the Table 4 with all CAN IDs you want to monitor
Step 6: Go to "Table 5 Configuration" if you need to configure more CAN IDs
CAN ID Monitoring Mode - Table 5 Configuration
RX Network: Select the source bus from which the frame is coming
Step 3: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 4: If CGW status is "Diag Com Mode", then skip this step
Else, Choose "Diag Com Mode START" and ckick on WRITE button
Step 6: Go to Activation Tab to start ID Monitoring mode
CAN ID: To enter a CAN ID with 11 bits, use following format: 000004D8
CAN ID: To enter a CAN ID with 29 bits, use following format 18DAF1D4
Step 1: Fill-up the Table 5 with all CAN IDs you want to monitor
CAN ID Monitoring Mode - Activation / Deactivation
Step 1: Start Diag Session by clicking on START button
Step 2: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 3: If CGW status is "Diag Com Mode", then skip this step
Else, Choose "Diag Com Mode START" and ckick on WRITE button
Step 4: Click on START CAN ID Monitoring mode
Step 1: Start Diag Session by clicking on START button
Step 2: Unlock the Gateway by clicking on the yellow padlock button
Step 3: Click on STOP CAN ID Monitoring mode
Number of received frames with CRC error
It is not possible to screen clearly within DDT which port is UDP port, and which port is TCP port
In order to differenciate TCP and UDP ports, you have to use the TCP and UDP counters
TCP ports are screened first, followed by UDP ports
Example: If TCP port counter indicates 3 and UDP port counter indicates 2, the first 3 ports are TCP, the 2 last ports are UDP
GW package signature Violation for FOTA
Secureboot Application digital signature Violation counter
Secureboot Configuration digital signature Violation counter
Back to Idle request response from FOTA-TECU
Back to Idle request to FOTA-TECU
Back to Idle request from FOTA-CLIENT
Change to "Copying in progress" for Configuration
Back to Idle request response from FOTA-TECU
Check Logical Block DTC request response
Change to "Copying in progress" for Configuration response
Current logical block signature read request
Default session request response from FOTA-TECU
Default session request response to FOTA-CLIENT
FOTA session request response from FOTA-TECU
FOTA session request response to FOTA-CLIENT
Menu package distribution start request response
Menu package distribution reqult request response
Request Transfert Exit response to FOTA-CLIENT
Request Transfert Exit response from FOTA-TECU
Number of Target ECU package blocks
Target ECU integrity check NOK result
Target ECU Atribute Report request response
Target ECU integrity check OK result
